Each process is described below with the exact commands run, key parameters, and links to external tool documentation.
Cartesian product expansion: The each qualifiers on rep, nqtl, effect, and h2 in the Nextflow process definitions create all parameter combinations. Every PLINK fileset from Phase 1 is crossed with every combination of replicate number, number of QTL, effect size distribution, and heritability value, so downstream processes run independently for each point in the parameter grid.

title: Phase 2: Trait Simulation
text:
1. PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL
Source: modules/python/simulate_effects_global/main.nf
Selects random causal variants from the CV pool (the LD-pruned variant set produced by PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF) and assigns effect sizes. This process executes the bin/create_causal_vars.py script. Using the CV pool rather than the marker SNP set allows causal variants to have lower MAF or higher LD than the GWA mapping panel, providing a more realistic simulation of causal architecture.
Command:
python create_causal_vars.py CV_TO_SIMS.bim ${nqtl} ${effect} ${rep}
mv causal_vars.txt causal.variants.sim.${nqtl}.${rep}.txt

Script functions







Function
Description




load_strain_set_variants()
Reads marker IDs from column 2 of the .bim file


select_variants()
Random sample of nQTL markers without replacement via rng.choice (seeded Generator)


simulate_effect_gamma()
Gamma(shape=0.4, scale=1.66) distribution with random sign (+1 or -1); uses seeded Generator


simulate_effect_uniform()
Uniform(low, high) distribution with random sign (+1 or -1); uses seeded Generator









NotePython dependency versions



create_causal_vars.py depends on numpy and pandas. The exact versions validated with this pipeline are recorded in requirements.txt at the repository root (numpy==2.2.3, pandas==2.2.3) and correspond to the packages present in the andersenlab/numpy:20250506 container. These versions are also captured at runtime in each task’s versions.yml output, so pipeline execution logs carry full library-version provenance.
For local development outside the container:
pip install -r requirements.txt




Effect type selection
The third command-line argument determines which effect size distribution is used:

gamma keyword — calls simulate_effect_gamma() with default shape=0.4 and scale=1.66
low-high range string (e.g. 0.4-0.9) — calls simulate_effect_uniform() with the parsed low and high bounds



Inputs / Outputs








Direction
File
Description




Input
CV_TO_SIMS.bim
PLINK marker information file from the CV pool (PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF, Phase 1); variant IDs in column 2


Input
nqtl (val)
Number of causal variants to select


Input
effect (val)
Effect size specification: gamma or low-high range


Input
rep (val)
Replicate number; used as RNG seed and in output filename


Output
causal.variants.sim.${nqtl}.${rep}.txt
Space-separated file: marker ID (col 1) and effect size (col 2), one row per causal variant









NoteReproducibility: RNG seeding



create_causal_vars.py seeds NumPy’s np.random.default_rng(rep) at startup, so identical rep values always produce the same causal variant selection and effect sizes. The seed value (rep) is stored as sim_seed in the trait metadata Parquet file for post-hoc verification.




Example output
From test data (data/test_data/.../PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL/causal.variants.sim.5.1.txt) with nQTL=5 and effect=gamma:
2:14378543 0.38901223435837223
2:7459092 -4.532985639400811
3:1537674 -0.0063686130105886415
4:16212978 0.47805937866664927
2:13733845 -0.03046281285149475
Each line contains a marker ID in CHROM:POS format and its assigned effect size (sign indicates direction).

title: Phase 2: Trait Simulation
text:
2. GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES
Source: modules/gcta/simulate_phenotypes/main.nf
Uses GCTA’s GWAS Simulation functionality to simulate quantitative phenotypes from the causal variants selected in step 1.
Command:
gcta64 --bfile CV_TO_SIMS \
     --simu-qt \
     --simu-causal-loci ${causal_variants} \
     --simu-hsq ${h2} \
     --simu-rep ${rep} \
     --autosome-num 6 \
     --thread-num 1 \
     --out ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_sims







Flag
Purpose




--simu-qt
Simulate quantitative traits


--simu-causal-loci
Causal variants file from step 1 (marker IDs and effect sizes)


--simu-hsq
Target heritability (h2); from the --h2 pipeline parameter, crossed in via each h2


--simu-rep ${rep}
Number of phenotype replicates GCTA generates in a single call; used as an RNG-advancement workaround — see note below


--autosome-num 6
C. elegans has 6 chromosomes (5 autosomes + X)


--thread-num 1
Pinned to 1 thread for deterministic floating-point results; multi-threaded BLAS introduces non-deterministic reduction order









NoteReplicate RNG strategy: --simu-rep ${rep} with awk trimming



GCTA has no --seed flag. When pipeline replicates run in parallel (as is typical on an HPC cluster), tasks starting near-simultaneously can receive the same time-based seed and therefore produce identical phenotype draws — defeating the purpose of replication.
Workaround: Pass --simu-rep ${rep} instead of --simu-rep 1. With --simu-rep N, GCTA generates N independent phenotype columns in the output .phen file (columns: FID IID pheno1 pheno2 ... phenoN). Because each additional column consumes more of GCTA’s internal RNG, replicate N draws from a different position in the random sequence even if two tasks share the same initial seed.
In-process trimming: Immediately after the GCTA call, an awk step extracts only the N-th phenotype column and overwrites the .phen file in place:
awk -v col=$((${rep} + 2)) '{print $1, $2, $col}' sims.phen > sims.phen.tmp
mv sims.phen.tmp sims.phen
The column index is rep + 2 because column 1 is FID, column 2 is IID, and the phenotype columns begin at column 3. This trims the file back to the standard 3-column format (FID IID value) before it is emitted, so no downstream consumers require any changes.
The .par file is unaffected — GCTA always outputs one row per causal variant regardless of --simu-rep N.
Edge case rep=1: col = 1 + 2 = 3. The awk step extracts the only column that exists — a no-op that preserves identical behaviour to the previous --simu-rep 1 setting.



Inputs / Outputs








Direction
File
Description




Input
CV_TO_SIMS.bed/.bim/.fam
CV pool PLINK binary fileset (from PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL.out.cv_plink); contains genotypes for all causal variants


Input
causal.variants.sim.${nqtl}.${rep}.txt
Causal variants file from step 1


Input
h2 (val)
Target heritability (crossed in via each h2)


Output
*_sims.phen
Simulated phenotype values (FID, IID, phenotype)


Output
*_sims.par
Simulation parameters (QTL, RefAllele, Frequency, Effect)









NoteDatabase migration: DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA



The .phen and .par outputs are captured by the DB path before the mode-crossing (GCTA_MAKE_GRM). DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA stores trait metadata, causal variants, and pre-upscaled phenotype values in the database for future offline variance-explained estimation. The phenotype values passed to PLINK_UPDATE_BY_H2 and ultimately to GWAS mapping may differ (upscaled by check_vp.py), but the ANOVA SS ratio used for offline estimation is scale-invariant.
Trait ID generation: Each simulated trait is assigned a deterministic 20-character SHA-256 hash identifier (trait_id). The ID is the second level of the canonical three-level hash hierarchy used throughout the database:
marker_set_id  (SHA-256 of "v=2|population=...|maf=...|species=...|vcf_release_id=...|ms_ld=...")
  └── trait_id (SHA-256 of "v=1|parent={marker_set_hash}|nqtl=...|effect=...|rep=...|h2=...")
        └── mapping_id (SHA-256 of "v=1|parent={trait_hash}|algorithm=...|pca=...")
The trait_id is computed in write_trait_data.R as:
ms_id    <- generate_marker_set_id(opt$group, as.numeric(opt$maf),
                                   opt$species, opt$vcf_release_id,
                                   as.numeric(opt$ms_ld))
trait    <- generate_trait_id(ms_id$hash, opt$nqtl, opt$effect, opt$rep, opt$h2)
trait_id <- trait$hash  # 20-character lowercase hex string
The hash is derived from this input string, where h2 and maf are serialized with sprintf("%.10f", as.numeric(...)) and nqtl/rep are serialized with as.integer():
v=1|parent={marker_set_hash}|nqtl={nqtl}|effect={effect}|rep={rep}|h2={h2}
The human-readable trait_hash_string and the parent marker_set_id are stored once in the trait metadata file (within the traits/ directory). The short 20-character trait_id hash serves as the join key in data files and is passed as the parent hash when generating downstream mapping_id values in Phase 3.




Example .par output
From test data (data/test_data/.../GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES/5_1_0.2_0.05_gamma_ce.96.allout15_irrepressible.grosbeak_sims.par):
QTL RefAllele   Frequency   Effect
2:7459092   A   0.0520833   -4.53299
2:13733845  A   0.125   -0.0304628
2:14378543  A   0.104167    0.389012
3:1537674   G   0.125   -0.00636861
4:16212978  T   0.0625  0.478059
The .par file has a header and four columns:

QTL — marker ID in CHROM:POS format
RefAllele — reference allele at the causal locus
Frequency — allele frequency in the strain set
Effect — effect size used in the simulation



Example .phen output
First 10 lines from 5_1_0.2_0.05_gamma_ce.96.allout15_irrepressible.grosbeak_sims.phen:
MY2713 MY2713 -6.04369
JU323 JU323 11.1615
XZ1734 XZ1734 4.15316
QG4080 QG4080 9.589
DL226 DL226 -4.79251
ECA2533 ECA2533 8.34439
XZ1672 XZ1672 5.00596
ED3046 ED3046 -2.37417
NIC1786 NIC1786 11.8277
NIC274 NIC274 -22.5104
The .phen file has no header and three space-separated columns:

Column 1 — Family ID (FID)
Column 2 — Individual ID (IID)
Column 3 — Simulated phenotype value

title: Phase 2: Trait Simulation
text:
3. PLINK_UPDATE_BY_H2
Source: modules/plink/update_by_h2/main.nf
Integrates the simulated phenotype values into the PLINK binary fileset and renames the output files to include all simulation parameters. This produces the phenotype-annotated PLINK files used by Phase 3 (GWAS Mapping).
Command:
plink --bfile TO_SIMS \
    --make-bed \
    --snps-only \
    --biallelic-only \
    --maf ${maf} \
    --set-missing-var-ids @:# \
    --geno \
    --recode \
    --out TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group} \
    --allow-extra-chr \
    --allow-no-sex \
    --pheno ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_sims.pheno



Flag
Purpose
Docs




--make-bed
Create PLINK binary fileset (.bed, .bim, .fam)
plink data


--snps-only
Exclude indels, retain only SNPs
plink filter


--biallelic-only
Exclude multiallelic sites
plink filter


--maf
Remove variants below the minor allele frequency threshold
plink filter


--set-missing-var-ids @:#
Assign IDs to variants missing an rsID, using CHROM:POS format
plink data


--geno
Exclude variants with missing genotype rates above the default threshold (0.1)
plink filter


--recode
Also produce text-format .ped/.map files
plink data


--allow-extra-chr
Permit non-standard chromosome codes
plink filter


--allow-no-sex
Handle samples with missing sex information
plink filter


--pheno
Embed simulated phenotype values into the .fam file
plink assoc









Note.phen to .pheno file rename



The GCTA simulation step outputs a .phen file, but the PLINK process input declaration expects a .pheno extension: ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_sims.pheno. Nextflow’s file staging handles this rename automatically when the output from GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES is wired into PLINK_UPDATE_BY_H2.








NoteRe-applied filtering flags



The filtering flags (--snps-only, --biallelic-only, --maf, --set-missing-var-ids, --geno) are the same as those used in Phase 1’s PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF / PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF. They are re-applied here for consistency across all PLINK commands in the pipeline, even though the input data has already been filtered.



Inputs / Outputs








Direction
File
Description




Input
TO_SIMS.bed/.bim/.fam
PLINK binary fileset from Phase 1


Input
*_sims.pheno
Simulated phenotype file (renamed from .phen)


Input
*_sims.par
Simulation parameter file (passed through)


Output
TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}.bed/.bim/.fam
Phenotype-annotated PLINK binary fileset


Output
TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}.map/.ped
PLINK text-format fileset


Output
TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}.nosex/.log
PLINK auxiliary outputs


Output
*_sims.pheno + *_sims.par
Phenotype and parameter files (passed through)





Output naming convention
Output files follow the pattern TO_SIMS_{nqtl}_{rep}_{h2}_{maf}_{effect}_{group}, encoding all simulation parameters in the filename:

nqtl — number of simulated QTL
rep — replicate number
h2 — target heritability
maf — minor allele frequency threshold
effect — effect size distribution (gamma or range)
group — strain set name

title: Functional Validation: Non-Marker Causal Variants
text:
The feature/add-nonmarker-cvs branch extends the pipeline to allow causal variants (CVs) to be drawn from a separately-configured variant pool that is independent of the GWA marker SNP set. Previously all causal variants had to be present in the marker SNP set — they were the same PLINK binary. This restriction was lifted by introducing a second, parallel PLINK run (the CV pool) for each strain group, whose MAF and LD thresholds can be set independently of the GWA marker set.
This branch is 22 commits ahead of feature-database-integration (13 step-branch merges, fixup commits, and new test data). Changes touch: the strainfile format, CLI parameters, the PLINK module, main.nf channel routing, Python effect simulation, GCTA phenotype inputs, the R hash framework (database.R), three DB-write R scripts and their Nextflow modules, and the test suite.
This notebook covers:

Strainfile format (2-column → 6-column)
Marker set ID hash — v=2 function demonstration (live R evaluation)
Dual PLINK pipeline wiring
CV binary routing through simulation
Unit test results
Pipeline stub runs (single-species test and three-species test_three_species)

title: Functional Validation: Non-Marker Causal Variants
text:
2. Strainfile Format

Before (2-column, feature-database-integration)
The old format was a space-separated file with no header. Each line had a group name and a comma-separated strain list. VCF source, species, MAF threshold, and LD threshold were all supplied via CLI parameters (--vcf, --species, --maf, --ms_ld).
ce.test.200strains AB1,BRC20067,BRC20263,...


After (6-column TSV, this branch)
The new format is a tab-separated file with a header row. Each row is a complete, self-contained strain group specification. The --vcf, --species, --maf, --ms_maf, and --ms_ld CLI parameters are removed.
group   species vcf ms_maf  ms_ld   strains
ce.test.200strains  c_elegans   data/test/test.20210901.vcf.gz  0.05    0.8 AB1,BRC20067,...
Column definitions:








Column
Type
Description




group
string
Unique strain group identifier; used in file naming and DB keys


species
string
c_elegans, c_briggsae, or c_tropicalis; controls CaeNDR URL construction


vcf
string
8-digit CaeNDR release date or path to a VCF file


ms_maf
float
Minor allele frequency threshold for the marker SNP set


ms_ld
float
LD R² pruning threshold for the marker SNP set


strains
string
Comma-separated strain list





Three-species strainfile
The new test_three_species profile uses data/test/test_strains_three_species.txt with 9 groups spanning all three supported Caenorhabditis species:
group   species vcf ms_maf  ms_ld   strains
ce.test c_elegans   data/test/test_ce.20250625.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 AB1,BBA2,...
ce.test.popA    c_elegans   data/test/test_ce.20250625.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 AB1,BBA2,...
ce.test.popB    c_elegans   data/test/test_ce.20250625.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 ECA1937,...
cb.test c_briggsae  data/test/test_cb.20250626.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 AF16,BRC20069,...
cb.test.popA    c_briggsae  data/test/test_cb.20250626.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 AF16,...
cb.test.popB    c_briggsae  data/test/test_cb.20250626.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 ECA1143,...
ct.test c_tropicalis    data/test/test_ct.20250627.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 BRC20391,...
ct.test.popA    c_tropicalis    data/test/test_ct.20250627.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 BRC20391,...
ct.test.popB    c_tropicalis    data/test/test_ct.20250627.vcf.gz   0.05    0.8 JU2505,...
Because species and vcf are per-row, a single pipeline invocation can mix C. elegans, C. briggsae, and C. tropicalis groups.

section: 3. Marker Set ID — v=2 Hash Function
text:
3. Marker Set ID — v=2 Hash Function
The marker set ID hash was upgraded from v=1 (2 parameters: population, maf) to v=2 (5 parameters: population, maf, species, vcf_release_id, ms_ld). The version prefix is encoded in the hash input string, making v=1 and v=2 hashes structurally incompatible — any existing DB built with v=1 hashes must be regenerated.

Sys.setenv(NEMASCAN_R_LIB = normalizePath("../R"))
source("../R/setup.R")

Some features are not enabled in this build of Arrow. Run `arrow_info()` for more information.


The repository you retrieved Arrow from did not include all of Arrow's features.
You can install a fully-featured version by running:
`install.packages('arrow', repos = 'https://apache.r-universe.dev')`.



Attaching package: 'arrow'


The following object is masked from 'package:utils':

    timestamp


Loading required package: DBI



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:data.table':

    between, first, last


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

# v=2: now includes species, vcf_release_id, ms_ld
ms <- generate_marker_set_id("ce.test", 0.05, "c_elegans", "20220216", 0.8)
ms$hash         # → "db39e634ef0bfa76e1bd"

[1] "db39e634ef0bfa76e1bd"

ms$hash_string  # → "v=2|population=ce.test|maf=0.0500000000|..."

[1] "v=2|population=ce.test|maf=0.0500000000|species=c_elegans|vcf_release_id=20220216|ms_ld=0.8000000000"



# Show trait and mapping ID chain
tr  <- generate_trait_id(ms$hash, 5, "gamma", 1, 0.8)
map <- generate_mapping_id(tr$hash, "inbred", TRUE)

cat("marker_set_id :", ms$hash, "\n")

marker_set_id : db39e634ef0bfa76e1bd 

cat("trait_id      :", tr$hash, "\n")

trait_id      : aadfdfe371a917896ad6 

cat("mapping_id    :", map$hash, "\n")

mapping_id    : c7e06e757cd1102e6092 



# Sensitivity: changing species changes the marker set hash
ms_cb <- generate_marker_set_id("ce.test", 0.05, "c_briggsae", "20220216", 0.8)
cat("c_elegans hash :", ms$hash, "\n")

c_elegans hash : db39e634ef0bfa76e1bd 

cat("c_briggsae hash:", ms_cb$hash, "\n")

c_briggsae hash: 9c823083ae97aa17bc39 

cat("hashes equal?  :", ms$hash == ms_cb$hash, "\n")  # → FALSE

hashes equal?  : FALSE 



# CV parameters are NOT in the hash — runs differing only in cv_maf/cv_ld reuse the same marker set record
ms2 <- generate_marker_set_id("ce.test", 0.05, "c_elegans", "20220216", 0.8)
cat("same hash on rerun?:", ms$hash == ms2$hash, "\n")  # → TRUE

same hash on rerun?: TRUE 








Important



CV parameters are not included in the marker set ID. Runs differing only in --cv_maf / --cv_ld reuse the same marker SNP set record. This is intentional — the GWA marker panel is independent of which variants are drawn as causal.

title: Functional Validation: Non-Marker Causal Variants
text:
4. Dual PLINK Pipeline

Aliased module imports
main.nf imports PLINK_RECODE_VCF twice under different aliases:
include { PLINK_RECODE_VCF as PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF } from './modules/plink/recode_vcf/main'
include { PLINK_RECODE_VCF as PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF } from './modules/plink/recode_vcf/main'


Channel fan-out
After VCF renaming, the renamed VCF is forked with .tap{} before the two parallel PLINK calls to avoid queue-channel double-consumption:
BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS.out.vcf
    .tap { ch_renamed_for_ms }
    .tap { ch_renamed_for_cv }

// Marker SNP set — uses per-group ms_maf and ms_ld from strainfile
PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF(
    ch_renamed_for_ms,
    ch_mito_num,
    ch_sf.ms_maf_vals.map { meta, ms_maf -> ms_maf },
    ch_sf.ms_ld_vals.map  { meta, ms_ld  -> ms_ld  }
)

// Causal variant pool — uses global cv_maf/cv_ld (or per-group ms_maf fallback)
PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF(
    ch_renamed_for_cv,
    ch_mito_num,
    ch_sf.cv_maf_vals.map { meta, cv_maf_eff -> cv_maf_eff },
    Channel.value(cv_ld)
)


Module change: each maf → val maf
The PLINK module input was changed from each maf (which creates a Cartesian product over a list of MAF values) to val maf (which receives a single pre-resolved value per upstream item). A new val ld input was added for the LD threshold:
// modules/plink/recode_vcf/main.nf
input:
tuple val(meta), path(vcf), path(vcf_index)
val mito_name
val maf          // was: each maf — val runs once per upstream item (no Cartesian product)
val ld           // LD R² threshold for --indep-pairwise; was hardcoded 0.8


Data-flow diagram
VCF (per group)
  │
  ▼
BCFTOOLS_EXTRACT_STRAINS → BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS
                                        │
                    ┌───────────────────┤ .tap{}
                    ▼                   ▼
           ch_renamed_for_ms   ch_renamed_for_cv
                    │                   │
                    ▼                   ▼
        PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF   PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF
        (ms_maf, ms_ld)        (cv_maf, cv_ld)
                    │                   │
              TO_SIMS.*           CV_TO_SIMS.*
        (GWA marker set)        (CV variant pool)


Process selector (nextflow.config)
ext.tag disambiguates SLURM job names when both aliases run for the same group:
process {
    withName: 'PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF' {
        ext.tag = { "${meta.id}_MS" }
    }
    withName: 'PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF' {
        ext.tag = { "${meta.id}_CV" }
    }
}

title: Changelog
text: All notable changes to nemascan-sims-nf are recorded here. The project does not use version tags; entries are organized by development phase and date.

title: Changelog
text:
[Unreleased] — March 2026

Fixed

Missing versions.yml in DB migration modules (fix/versions_yml_db_migration, 2026-03-05): Added versions.yml output to all 7 modules/db_migration/*/main.nf processes (write_marker_set, write_genotype_matrix, write_gwa_to_db, write_trait_data, aggregate_metadata, analyze_qtl, assess_sims). The pattern was established across all other module families (bcftools, GCTA, PLINK, R, Python) but had not been carried forward when the DB migration layer was added. All 7 processes now capture the R version and wire their versions output channel into ch_versions in main.nf.



Fixed
<<<<<<< HEAD - Pipeline configuration gaps (fix/pipeline_configuration_gaps, 2026-03-05): - Added --output_dir parameter for stable, deterministic output directory naming. The date-based default (Analysis_Results-{date}) is preserved when --output_dir is not specified. - Added nextflow_schema.json — machine-readable parameter schema covering all pipeline parameters, compatible with nf-core tooling and IDE parameter validation. - Documented previously undocumented parameters --alpha and --legacy_assess in the README parameters table and --help output. - Parameterized hardcoded Rockfish cluster paths in conf/rockfish.config: --slurm_account (replaces hardcoded eande106 in clusterOptions) and --scratch_dir (replaces hardcoded /scratch4/eande106 in workflow.workDir). All Rockfish infrastructure params are now overridable without editing the config file. ======= - Stochastic script reproducibility (fix/rand-seed-stochastic-scripts, 2026-03-05): causal variant selection and effect size simulation are now fully reproducible across pipeline runs: - bin/create_causal_vars.py seeds np.random.default_rng(rep) at startup; all stochastic calls (rng.choice, rng.gamma, rng.uniform) use the seeded Generator. Previously, np.random.default_rng() was called without a seed, making results non-deterministic. - bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R calls set.seed(as.integer(rep)) before any stochastic draws (rgamma, sample, runif). - Both Nextflow modules (PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL, R_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL, R_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_LOCAL) now pass ${rep} as the seed argument to the scripts. - trait_metadata_schema() gains a new sim_seed (int32) field; write_trait_metadata() stores the seed used for each simulated trait, enabling exact post-hoc reproduction without the Nextflow cache. >>>>>>> origin/reproducibility-updates


Fixed

Pipeline configuration gaps (fix/pipeline_configuration_gaps, 2026-03-05):

Added --output_dir parameter for stable, deterministic output directory naming. The date-based default (Analysis_Results-{date}) is preserved when --output_dir is not specified.
Added nextflow_schema.json — machine-readable parameter schema covering all pipeline parameters, compatible with nf-core tooling and IDE parameter validation.
Documented previously undocumented parameters --alpha and --legacy_assess in the README parameters table and --help output.
Parameterized hardcoded Rockfish cluster paths in conf/rockfish.config: --slurm_account (replaces hardcoded eande106 in clusterOptions) and --scratch_dir (replaces hardcoded /scratch4/eande106 in workflow.workDir). All Rockfish infrastructure params are now overridable without editing the config file.




Fixed

Stochastic script reproducibility (fix/rand-seed-stochastic-scripts, 2026-03-05): causal variant selection and effect size simulation are now fully reproducible across pipeline runs:

bin/create_causal_vars.py seeds np.random.default_rng(rep) at startup; all stochastic calls (rng.choice, rng.gamma, rng.uniform) use the seeded Generator. Previously, np.random.default_rng() was called without a seed, making results non-deterministic.
bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R calls set.seed(as.integer(rep)) before any stochastic draws (rgamma, sample, runif).
Both Nextflow modules (PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL, R_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL, R_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_LOCAL) now pass ${rep} as the seed argument to the scripts.
trait_metadata_schema() gains a new sim_seed (int32) field; write_trait_metadata() stores the seed used for each simulated trait, enabling exact post-hoc reproduction without the Nextflow cache.




Added

Non-marker causal variants (feature/add-nonmarker-cvs, 2026-03-03): causal variants can now be drawn from a separately-configured variant pool rather than the marker SNP set. Key changes:

Strainfile format extended to 6 columns: group, species, vcf, ms_maf, ms_ld, strains. Species, VCF source, and marker-set thresholds are now per-group in the strainfile; --vcf, --maf, --species, --vcf_release_id, --ms_maf, and --ms_ld CLI parameters removed.
New CLI parameters --cv_maf (default: each group’s ms_maf) and --cv_ld (default: 0.8) configure the causal variant pool MAF and LD thresholds independently of the marker SNP set.
Two parallel PLINK runs per group: PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF (marker SNP set, drives GWA) and PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF (CV pool, drives causal variant selection). Mixed-species runs supported in a single pipeline invocation.
PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL now selects causal variants from CV_TO_SIMS.bim (CV pool BIM); GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES now uses --bfile CV_TO_SIMS.
Marker set ID upgraded to v=2: SHA-256 now encodes population, maf, species, vcf_release_id, and ms_ld. CV parameters (cv_maf, cv_ld) are intentionally excluded from the hash.
marker_set_metadata.parquet gains three new columns: species (utf8), vcf_release_id (utf8), ms_ld (float64).
DB write scripts (write_marker_set.R, write_genotype_matrix.R, write_gwa_to_db.R) and their Nextflow modules updated to accept and pass --species, --vcf_release_id, --ms_ld.
aggregate_metadata.R fallback path updated to emit NA for the three new columns when no metadata sidecars exist.




Fixed

Python dependency pinning (fix/python-requirements, 2026-03-05): added requirements.txt at the repository root pinning numpy==2.2.3 and pandas==2.2.3 to the versions present in the andersenlab/numpy:20250506 container. The PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL versions.yml emission now also records numpy and pandas library versions alongside the Python interpreter version, providing runtime provenance in pipeline output logs.



Added

R package lockfile (fix/r_package_lockfile, 2026-03-05): renv.lock committed at the project root records the exact versions of all R packages installed in the andersenlab/r_packages:20250519 container. Provides a verifiable record of the software environment that produced simulation results and enables local environment reproduction via renv::restore(). scripts/generate_renv_lock.sh documents how to regenerate the lockfile when the container changes.



Added

Strainfile provenance in marker set metadata (fix/strain-file-not-hashed, 2026-03-05): marker_set_metadata.parquet now records two new columns for reproducibility auditing:

strainfile_hash (utf8) — SHA-256 hex digest of the complete strainfile, computed once at pipeline submission time (same value for all population groups in a run).
strain_list (utf8) — Comma-separated list of strain names used for the specific population group, derived from the strainfile at submission time.
Both columns are optional/NA-safe: queries against databases produced before this change return NULL without error.
Enables post-hoc verification that a database was produced with a particular strain set, and that the strainfile has not been modified between runs.




Changed

Mapping data Parquet schema: FK columns removed (reproducibility-updates, 2026-03-10): mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/data.parquet now contains only the five measurement columns (marker, AF1, BETA, SE, P). The three foreign-key columns previously stored in every data file have been relocated:

mapping_id — already encoded in the Hive directory path; DuckDB auto-promotes it as a virtual column when the directory tree is scanned, so no query changes are needed.
marker_set_id and trait_id — moved to mappings_metadata.parquet, accessible via the metadata DuckDB view. This removes constant-per-mapping columns from the millions-of-rows data files without losing any information.




Added

mappings_metadata.parquet sidecar file written to the database root by DB_MIGRATION_AGGREGATE_METADATA — provides a single queryable index of all mapping runs without scanning every Hive partition. (2026-03-02)

title: Changelog
text:
Phase 5 — Database Integration

PR #98 — Hash-based IDs (2026-03-01)
Replaced sequential or filename-derived identifiers with deterministic 12-character MD5 hex IDs, making all IDs stable across -resume runs.

Added

generate_trait_id(group, maf, nqtl, effect, rep, h2) — MD5 hash of concatenated parameters; used as the primary key for all per-trait Parquet files (traits/, causal_variants/, phenotypes/).
generate_mapping_id(trait_id, mode, type) — MD5 hash used as the Hive partition key under mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/.
generate_marker_set_id(population, maf, species, vcf_release_id, ms_ld) — SHA-256 hash (v=2) written to marker_set_metadata.parquet and used in the marker-set file names.
Unit tests for all three hash functions in test-hash_generation.R, with golden-value assertions to catch silent hash drift.



Changed

Mappings Parquet schema reduced to core columns; accessible metadata (population, MAF, effect, nQTL, h2, replicate) is no longer duplicated in the mappings table — it is retrievable via the trait ID foreign key.
Allele frequency (AF) column removed from the marker-set schema.
Algorithm names standardized to loco / inbred across all pipeline paths (both DB and legacy) to replace inconsistent earlier labels.
Trait storage tests updated to cover hash-based ID determinism and round-trip write/read.



Fixed

Query functions updated to remove the defunct AF join that referenced a column no longer in the schema.
Trait id generation corrected to use the hash framework (previously still used a non-hash method after the schema change).




PR #97 — Automated Cross-Validation Tests (2026-02-27)

Added

test-assessment_cross_validation.R — compares db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv (DB path) against simulation_assessment_results.tsv (legacy path), computing per-replicate detection concordance, designation concordance, and interval overlap (Jaccard score).
test-cross_validation.R — round-trip check comparing Parquet DB contents against raw .fastGWA/.mlma files in the Nextflow work directory.




PR #96 — Phase 5: Genotype & Trait Storage (2026-02-21)
Extended the Parquet database to capture the full data needed for offline variance-explained estimation and richer downstream queries.

Added

Genotype matrix storage — write_genotype_matrix() / read_genotype_matrix() in R/database.R; long-format Parquet files ({pop}_{maf}_genotypes.parquet) written to marker_sets/.
DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX Nextflow module (modules/db_migration/write_genotype_matrix/).
Trait data storage — three write functions (write_trait_metadata(), write_causal_variants(), write_phenotype_data()) and corresponding Parquet schemas in R/database.R.
DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA Nextflow module (modules/db_migration/write_trait_data/); uses .merge() to align GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES output channels by emission order.
Phenotype stored in phenotypes/ is the pre-upscaling GCTA .phen output (before check_vp.py), making the ANOVA SS ratio for offline variance-explained scale-invariant.
Unit tests in test-genotype_storage.R and test-trait_storage.R.
Test fixture: tests/fixtures/test_genotype_matrix.tsv (5 markers × 3 strains) and tests/fixtures/test_sims.phen (3-strain GCTA .phen file).



Changed

var.exp column removed from mappings_schema() and prepare_mapping_data(); it was always NA in the DB path. Raw data for offline estimation is now available in traits/, causal_variants/, and phenotypes/.
All DB write functions default to overwrite = TRUE to handle non-atomic arrow::write_parquet() writes — a crashed task leaves a partial file; retry with overwrite = TRUE replaces it cleanly.
Narrow glob _markers\\.parquet$ used in R/queries.R to exclude genotype files from the markers DuckDB view (genotype files share the marker_sets/ directory but have a different schema).
Database integration workflow made the default path; legacy R assessment is now opt-in via --legacy_assess.




PR #95 — Documentation Site (2026-02-18)

Added

Quarto website in docs/ published to GitHub Pages at https://andersenlab.github.io/nemascan-sims-nf/.
One page per pipeline phase: Marker Set Generation, Trait Simulation, GWAS Mapping, QTL Detection & Assessment, Database Structure.
Development guide covering local rendering, publishing, testing, HPC execution, and project structure.




PR #93 — Cross-Validation Framework (2026-02-17)

Added

cross-validation.qmd documentation page explaining concordance methodology.
Cross-validation assessment script computing concordance scores (detection, designation, interval overlap) between the DB path and legacy R path.

title: Phase 1: Marker Set Generation
text:
The first phase of the pipeline transforms a multi-sample VCF into LD-pruned PLINK binary filesets and a numeric genotype matrix. These outputs feed into phenotype simulation (Phase 2) and GWAS mapping (Phase 3).
The processing flow is:
VCF
 ├─ LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO ──► contig_mapping.tsv, contigs.txt
 │                                    │
 ▼                                    │
 BCFTOOLS_EXTRACT_STRAINS             │
 ▼                                    │
 BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS ◄─────────────┘
 ▼
 PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF ────────► TO_SIMS.* binaries + markers.txt (marker SNP set)
 PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF ────────► CV_TO_SIMS.* binaries (causal variant pool)
 │                                    │
 ▼                                    ▼
 (to Phase 2/3)          BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX ──► genotype matrix TSV
                                       │
                          (DB path — see callout notes below)
                          ├─ DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET ──► marker_sets/{id}_markers.parquet
                          │    (requires .bim + n_indep_tests from EIGEN)
                          └─ DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX ──► genotypes/{id}_genotypes.parquet
Each process is described below with the exact commands run, key parameters, and links to external tool documentation.

Each process is described below with the exact commands run, key parameters, and links to external tool documentation.

title: Phase 1: Marker Set Generation
text:
LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO (upstream helper)
Source: modules/local/get_contig_info/main.nf
Parses ##contig header lines from the VCF to build a name-to-numeric mapping (e.g. I → 1, II → 2, MtDNA → 7). This mapping is required because PLINK and GCTA expect numeric chromosome identifiers.
Commands:
# Extract ##contig headers and build tab-separated mapping (name → number)
zcat input.vcf.gz | head -n 200 | grep "##contig" | \
    awk 'BEGIN{COUNT=1}{split($1,A,"="); split(A[3],B,","); CHROM=B[1];
         printf "%s\t%i\n", CHROM, COUNT; COUNT=COUNT+1}' > contig_mapping.tsv

# Extract just the chromosome names
cut -f 1 contig_mapping.tsv > contigs.txt
Outputs:







File
Description




contig_mapping.tsv
Tab-separated file: chromosome name (col 1) and numeric ID (col 2)


contigs.txt
One chromosome name per line (used by main.nf for mito filtering and chromosome list)



Used by:

BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS — consumes contig_mapping.tsv as the rename mapping file
main.nf workflow logic — reads contigs.txt to identify the mitochondrial chromosome name and generate the list of autosomal chromosomes

title: Phase 1: Marker Set Generation
text:
1. BCFTOOLS_EXTRACT_STRAINS
Source: modules/bcftools/extract_strains/main.nf
Subsets the VCF to only the strains in the current strain set and removes any sites with missing genotypes. This produces a clean, strain-specific VCF for downstream processing.
Commands:
# Subset samples and filter missing sites, then index
bcftools view --threads ${task.cpus} -s ${strains} -O u ${vcf} | \
bcftools filter -i N_MISSING=0 -O z -o ${group}.vcf.gz
bcftools index --tbi ${group}.vcf.gz



Command
Purpose
Docs




bcftools view -s
Subset to specified sample names
bcftools view


bcftools filter -i N_MISSING=0
Keep only sites where no sample has a missing genotype
bcftools filter


bcftools index --tbi
Create a tabix (.tbi) index for the output VCF
bcftools index



Key flags:

-O u — pipe as uncompressed BCF (faster for piping between bcftools commands)
-O z — write bgzf-compressed VCF output

Inputs / Outputs:








Direction
File
Description




Input
*.vcf.gz + .tbi
Raw input VCF (original, before chromosome renaming)


Input
strains (val)
Comma-separated list of strain names


Output
${group}.vcf.gz + .tbi
Strain-subset, no-missing-site VCF with tabix index

title: Phase 1: Marker Set Generation
text:
2. BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS
Source: modules/bcftools/rename_chroms/main.nf
Renames chromosome identifiers from text (I, II, III, …) to numeric (1, 2, 3, …) using the mapping file produced by LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO. Numeric chromosome names are required by PLINK and GCTA.
Commands:
# Stream VCF, rename chromosomes, compress and index
bcftools view --threads ${task.cpus} -O u ${vcf} | \
bcftools annotate --rename-chrs chr_mapping.tsv -O z -o ${id}_renamed.vcf.gz
bcftools index --tbi ${id}_renamed.vcf.gz



Command
Purpose
Docs




bcftools view

title: Phase 1: Marker Set Generation
text:
3. PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF / PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF
Source: modules/plink/recode_vcf/main.nf (aliased twice in main.nf)
The same module is imported under two alias names and called in parallel for each strain group:

PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF — marker SNP set run. Uses per-group ms_maf and ms_ld thresholds from the strainfile. Outputs TO_SIMS.* files used for GRM construction and GWA mapping (Phase 3).
PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF — causal variant pool run. Uses params.cv_maf (defaults to the group’s ms_maf if not set) and params.cv_ld thresholds. Outputs CV_TO_SIMS.* files used for causal variant selection in Phase 2.

Both runs perform LD pruning and produce PLINK binary filesets through two sequential PLINK commands: first to identify LD-pruned variants, then to create the binary files using only those variants.

Command 1: LD pruning
plink --vcf recoded.vcf.gz \
    --snps-only \
    --biallelic-only \
    --maf ${maf} \
    --set-missing-var-ids @:# \
    --indep-pairwise 50 10 ${ld} \
    --geno \
    --not-chr ${mito_name} \
    --allow-extra-chr
This command computes pairwise LD and produces plink.prune.in (variant IDs to retain) and plink.prune.out (variant IDs to remove).



Flag
Purpose
Docs




--snps-only
Exclude indels, retain only SNPs
plink filter


--biallelic-only
Exclude multiallelic sites
plink filter


--maf ${maf}
Remove variants below the minor allele frequency threshold
plink filter


--set-missing-var-ids @:#
Assign IDs to variants missing an rsID, using CHROM:POS format
plink data


--indep-pairwise 50 10 ${ld}
Sliding-window LD pruning: window=50 variants, step=10, r2 threshold from ld input (per-group ms_ld for the MS run; cv_ld for the CV run)
plink LD


--geno
Exclude variants with missing genotype rates above the default threshold (0.1)
plink filter


--not-chr ${mito_name}
Exclude the mitochondrial chromosome
plink filter


--allow-extra-chr
Permit non-standard chromosome codes
plink filter





Command 2: Binary fileset creation
plink --vcf recoded.vcf.gz \
    --make-bed \
    --snps-only \
    --biallelic-only \
    --maf ${maf} \
    --set-missing-var-ids @:# \
    --extract plink.prune.in \
    --geno \
    --recode \
    --out TO_SIMS \
    --allow-extra-chr
Re-applies the same filtering flags for consistency and additionally:



Flag
Purpose
Docs




--make-bed
Create PLINK binary fileset (.bed, .bim, .fam)
plink data


--extract plink.prune.in
Retain only variants that passed LD pruning
plink data


--recode
Also produce text-format .ped/.map files
plink data


--out TO_SIMS
Set output file prefix
—





Post-processing: markers.txt
awk -F":" '$1=$1' OFS="\t" plink.prune.in | \
    sort -k1,1d -k2,2n > markers.txt
Converts the CHROM:POS variant IDs from plink.prune.in into a tab-separated CHROM  POS file, sorted by chromosome then position. This marker list is used by BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX to subset the VCF.


Inputs / Outputs








Direction
File
Description




Input

Chromosome-renamed, strain-subset VCF


Input

Numeric mitochondrial chromosome ID (looked up from LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO mapping using the --mito_name parameter; e.g., 7 for MtDNA)


Input
maf (val)
Minor allele frequency threshold (ms_maf for the MS run; cv_maf or fallback ms_maf for the CV run)


Input
ld (val)
LD R² pruning threshold (ms_ld for the MS run; cv_ld for the CV run)


Output (MS run)
TO_SIMS.bed/.bim/.fam
PLINK binary fileset for GRM construction and GWA mapping (Phase 3)


Output (MS run)
TO_SIMS.map/.ped
PLINK text-format fileset


Output (MS run)
TO_SIMS.nosex/.log
PLINK auxiliary outputs


Output (CV run)
CV_TO_SIMS.bed/.bim/.fam
PLINK binary fileset for causal variant selection (Phase 2)


Output (CV run)
CV_TO_SIMS.map/.ped
PLINK text-format fileset


Output (CV run)
CV_TO_SIMS.nosex/.log
PLINK auxiliary outputs


Output (MS run)
recoded.vcf.gz + .tbi
Copy of the input VCF (passed through for downstream use)


Output (MS run)
markers.txt
Tab-separated list of retained markers (CHROM, POS)









Note



The TO_SIMS.bim file (MS run output) is consumed by DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET, which writes the LD-pruned marker list to {db}/markers/marker_sets/{marker_set_id}_markers.parquet. marker_set_id is the 20-character SHA-256 hash (v=2) computed from (population, maf, species, vcf_release_id, ms_ld) by generate_marker_set_id() in R/database.R. DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET also requires n_indep_tests (number of independent tests from EIGEN analysis) before it can run; it stores this alongside the marker list in marker_set_metadata.parquet.
The CV_TO_SIMS.* binary (CV run output) is not written to the database. It is passed to Phase 2 (PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL) for causal variant selection, then forwarded to GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES as the --bfile source. Only the MS binary drives GWA mapping.

title: Phase 1: Marker Set Generation
text:
4. BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX
Source: modules/bcftools/create_genotype_matrix/main.nf
Creates a numeric genotype matrix (markers × strains) from the VCF, restricted to the LD-pruned marker set. Genotypes are encoded numerically for use in eigenvalue decomposition and downstream R analysis.
Commands:
# List and sort sample names
bcftools query -l ${vcf} | sort > sorted_samples.txt

# Extract genotypes for pruned markers, recode to numeric
bcftools view -v snps -S sorted_samples.txt -R ${markers} ${vcf} | \
    bcftools query --print-header -f '%CHROM\t%POS\t%REF\t%ALT[\t%GT]\n' | \
    sed 's/[[# 0-9]*]//g' | \   # clean header annotations
    sed 's/:GT//g' | \           # remove :GT suffix from sample names
    sed 's/0|0/-1/g' | \         # REF/REF homozygous → -1
    sed 's/1|1/1/g' | \          # ALT/ALT homozygous →  1
    sed 's/0|1/NA/g' | \         # heterozygous → NA
    sed 's/1|0/NA/g' | \         # heterozygous → NA
    sed 's/.|./NA/g' | \         # missing → NA
    sed 's/0\/0/-1/g' | \        # (unphased) REF/REF → -1
    sed 's/1\/1/1/g' | \         # (unphased) ALT/ALT →  1
    sed 's/0\/1/NA/g' | \        # (unphased) het → NA
    sed 's/1\/0/NA/g' | \        # (unphased) het → NA
    sed 's/.\/./NA/g' \          # (unphased) missing → NA
    > ${group}_${maf}_Genotype_Matrix.tsv



Command
Purpose
Docs




bcftools query -l
List sample names from the VCF header
bcftools query


bcftools view -v snps -S ... -R ...
Subset to SNPs, reorder samples, restrict to marker regions
bcftools view


bcftools query -f
Extract fields in a custom tab-separated format
bcftools query




Genotype encoding



Genotype
Encoding
Meaning




0\|0 or 0/0
-1
Homozygous reference


1\|1 or 1/1
1
Homozygous alternate


0\|1, 1\|0, 0/1, 1/0
NA
Heterozygous


.\|., ./.
NA
Missing



Design note: The encoding assumes inbred diploid organisms. C. elegans strains are expected to be homozygous, so heterozygous calls are treated as missing data (NA) rather than given an intermediate value.


Inputs / Outputs








Direction
File
Description




Input
recoded.vcf.gz + .tbi
Chromosome-renamed, strain-subset VCF


Input
markers.txt
LD-pruned marker positions from PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF


Output
${group}_${maf}_Genotype_Matrix.tsv
Tab-separated genotype matrix: rows are markers (CHROM, POS, REF, ALT), columns are strains









Note



This TSV is consumed by two downstream paths: the legacy path (R_GET_GCTA_INTERVALS, R_ASSESS_SIMS) uses it for variance explained estimation; the DB path (DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX) pivots it to long format and stores it in {db}/markers/genotypes/{marker_set_id}_genotypes.parquet for future offline analysis. marker_set_id is the 20-character SHA-256 hash of the population and MAF threshold, computed by generate_marker_set_id() in R/database.R.
